trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider PT-141 as a centrally-acting libido tool, not a vascular one — Trevor (GQppfX2TSz4 00:30:48.720): "melanoordin receptor agonist that acts centrally on the hypothalamus to spark actually spark sexual desire independent of any hormonal pathways" and Trevor (xVdEqtmWBKE 00:33:27.440): "It works on 100% of people. You're welcome, men and women." We'd ask the prescribing physician to start at Hunter's published floor — Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Standard starting dose: 1 mg = 0.2 mL = 20 units" and "Do NOT exceed 2 mg per dose" — and to weigh nausea risk because Hunter (same email): "Nausea: ~40%, Flushing: ~20%, Headaches: ~11%." Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:53.440): "a lot of women and sometimes men say like when they inject PT-141, they get nauseous and sick from it. My reason behind that is I think a lot of it is dosing it too high" — supports Trevor's microdose frame and Hunter's 1 mg start over higher boluses.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Nausea/flushing/headache load: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Nausea: ~40%", "Flushing: ~20%", "Headaches: ~11%"; Hunter (same email): "BP: temporary spike (~6 mm Hg systolic, 3 mm Hg diastolic) — returns to normal after 12 hours."
- Dose ceiling: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Do NOT exceed 2 mg per dose (minimize nausea + side effects)"; Trevor (1r5FQHIx5-o 00:33:42.640): "if you take too much, you look like a tomato. first of all and second of all makes you nauseated"; Trevor (GQppfX2TSz4 00:30:48.720): "if you take too much, it's going to make your stomach feel kind of crappy."
- Fasting interaction: Hunter email threadId 194f205727e4de34 (2025-02-10): "Melanocortin receptor peptides (like PT-141 or Melanotan 1/2)... While some folks find a fasted state enhances absorption, nausea can be a common side effect. And the nausea can be exacerbated by a fasted state."
- Hormone optimization is gating: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Optimize hormones — if T, E2, dopamine off, PT-141 won't work as well"; Hunter (same email): "Just like with GLP-1 peptides, people think PT-141 will do all the work for them. Doesn't work like that."
- Individual response varies: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "I personally LOVE using it, while my business partner Jay Campbell gets debilitating nausea after injecting."
- Storage: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Reconstituted: refrigerate, use within 90 days for best potency."
- HRV/early-cycle adaptation: Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 01:11:53.040) on listener Stacy: "My HRV dropped when I tried several different injections, PT-141, HGH. Have you seen experience this? Does your body eventually get used to it?" — Taylor: "Yes, that's very common... your body will get used to it."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Mechanism — all three frame PT-141 as centrally-acting on melanocortin receptors, not vascular. Trevor (GQppfX2TSz4 00:30:48.720): "melanoordin receptor agonist that acts centrally on the hypothalamus to spark actually spark sexual desire independent of any hormonal pathways"; Trevor (xVdEqtmWBKE 00:33:27.440): "It works on a neurological level" and "melanoorton receptor agonist. It doesn't work on the hormones. It works downstream"; Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "PT-141 = acts on nervous system, stimulates melanocortin receptors for sexual arousal (CENTRAL mechanism)"; Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:42.000): "melanotan 1 and PT-41 and melanotan are in similar pathway in families." Works in both sexes — Trevor (xVdEqtmWBKE 00:33:36.000): "It works on 100% of people. You're welcome, men and women"; Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Works for both men AND women." Side-effect profile — Hunter, Taylor, Trevor all surface nausea + flush from over-dosing. Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Nausea: ~40%"; Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:53.440): "a lot of women and sometimes men say like when they inject PT-141, they get nauseous and sick from it. My reason behind that is I think a lot of it is dosing it too high"; Trevor (GQppfX2TSz4 00:30:48.720): "causes a little bit of a skin flush for about 30 minutes. And if you take too much, it's going to make your stomach feel kind of crappy."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Dose framing differs sharply. Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06) gives an explicit per-event protocol — "Standard starting dose: 1 mg = 0.2 mL = 20 units", "Increase to 1.5-2 mg if needed based on response", "Do NOT exceed 2 mg per dose" — while Trevor (m8FwL9Xsk78 00:22:31.080) talks about "microdosing bremelanotide" as his preferred frame and (1r5FQHIx5-o 00:33:42.640) only says "if you take too much, you look like a tomato" without naming a number. Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:53.440) splits from Hunter on dose-spacing — Hunter (same 2025-02-06 email) says "For most users: once or twice per week", Taylor says "if you use it a little bit more frequently um and let your body get used to it, those symptoms kind of go away after a while." Route — Hunter's primary protocol is subQ injection (Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 2025-02-06: "Start: 1 mg subQ"), but Hunter is also developing a buccal strip — Hunter email threadId 197664a22d785112 (2025-06-12): "BioLongevity Labs, we're actively developing and testing several buccal and sublingual peptide products—including BPC-157 and PT-141 for rapid-onset effects." Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:35.000) demonstrates the buccal strip on-camera: "this is going to help absorb the peptide through the membrane of the roof of the mouth... it's going to bypass the liver and everything. And this is the PT-141" — using it "as a pre-workout" rather than for libido. Use-case framing — Hunter and Trevor frame PT-141 around libido/sexual function; Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:38.000) adds "Hunter and I are going to use it as a pre-workout instead of using Seialis today" and Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 01:54:16.040) adds "bremelanotide for Raynaud's works good, too" — neither use Hunter or Trevor surface as primary indications.

acute.dose: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Standard starting dose: 1 mg = 0.2 mL = 20 units"; Hunter (same email): "Start: 1 mg subQ. Increase to 1.5-2 mg if needed based on response." Hunter (same email): "Do NOT exceed 2 mg per dose (minimize nausea + side effects)."
acute.route: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Start: 1 mg subQ"; Hunter email threadId 197664a22d785112 (2025-06-12) developing alternative: "BioLongevity Labs, we're actively developing and testing several buccal and sublingual peptide products—including BPC-157 and PT-141 for rapid-onset effects"; Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:32.000) demonstrates the strip: "going to bypass the liver and everything. And this is the PT-141."
acute.frequency: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "For most users: once or twice per week." Hunter (same email): "Administer 1-4 hours BEFORE desired effects."
acute.duration: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Onset: 30-60 min after subQ injection"; Hunter (same email): "Effects last up to 16 HOURS"; Hunter (same email): "effects peak 2-6 hours."
acute.verbatim_citation: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Standard starting dose: 1 mg = 0.2 mL = 20 units" and "Do NOT exceed 2 mg per dose (minimize nausea + side effects)."

chronic.dose: Trevor (m8FwL9Xsk78 00:22:31.080): "microdosing bremelanotide" (no numeric dose stated by Trevor in surfaced transcripts); Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06) caps chronic use at "Do NOT exceed 2 mg per dose" with "once or twice per week" frequency.
chronic.route: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Start: 1 mg subQ" — subcutaneous primary; sublingual buccal in development per Hunter email threadId 197664a22d785112 (2025-06-12): "buccal and sublingual peptide products—including BPC-157 and PT-141 for rapid-onset effects."
chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "For most users: once or twice per week." Taylor counters with more-frequent low-dose adaptation — Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:55.440): "if you use it a little bit more frequently um and let your body get used to it, those symptoms kind of go away after a while."
chronic.duration: Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "Reconstituted: refrigerate, use within 90 days for best potency" (vial-cycle, not body-cycle); no expert in surfaced sources gives a chronic on-cycle/off-cycle duration for PT-141 specifically.
chronic.verbatim_citation: Trevor (m8FwL9Xsk78 00:22:31.080): "microdosing bremelanotide"; Trevor (xVdEqtmWBKE 00:33:27.440): "melanoorton receptor agonist. It doesn't work on the hormones. It works downstream. It taps the brain's libido centers." Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06): "For most users: once or twice per week."
chronic.timestamp: 00:22:31.080 (Trevor microdose framing); Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 (2025-02-06) for written protocol

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Trevor (GQppfX2TSz4 00:30:48.720) frames PT-141 as a fix for menopause-era libido without hormone shifts: "libido is a massive issue for women when they're going through pmenopause or menopause. So beyond lowd dose testosterone PT-141 this is a gamecher" and "for a lot of women, this is the missing key that restores a healthy libido. Like, hey, I'd like to have sex without significantly altering all of their hormonal profile and just demolishing their system." Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:48.000) on women's nausea pattern: "a lot of women and sometimes men say like when they inject PT-141, they get nauseous and sick from it. My reason behind that is I think a lot of it is dosing it too high." Taylor (VHZuOyJwopI 00:07:38.000) demonstrates a non-libido female use as pre-workout: "Hunter and I are going to use it as a pre-workout instead of using Seialis today." Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 01:54:16.040): "bremelanotide for Raynaud's works good, too." Listener-pattern from Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 01:11:53.040) — Stacy reports HRV drop on PT-141 + HGH; Taylor: "Yes, that's very common... your body will get used to it." Hunter clinical note (Hunter email threadId 194dd65935b19812 2025-02-06) cites the female trial signal: "Two Phase 3 trials (premenopausal women with HSDD) → statistically significant ↑ sexual desire" and "Pooled analysis 1.25/1.75 mg → statistically + clinically significant ↑ Satisfying Sexual Events (SSEs) per month."
